name: ROSAI-DORFMAN DISEASE

demographics.Etiology: reaction to infectious agents / autoimmune process
demographics.Histo: polymorphous infiltrate of lymphoplasmacytic cells and histiocytes of varying size
demographics.Age: primarily in children + young adults; M > F
demographics.Location: cerebral convexities, parasagittal, petroclival, suprasellar regions

dominant_finding: central low SI on T2WI ← release of free radicals by inflammatory macrophages
distribution: cerebral convexities, parasagittal, petroclival, suprasellar regions
discriminating_features: well-circumscribed dural-based iso- to hyperattenuating single / multiple masses
isointense on T1WI + iso- to hypointense on T2WI:
central low SI on T2WI ← release of free radicals by inflammatory macrophages

general: well-circumscribed dural-based iso- to hyperattenuating single / multiple masses
isointense on T1WI + iso- to hypointense on T2WI:
central low SI on T2WI ← release of free radicals by inflammatory macrophages
